narration: A dummy node gives the merged list a safe starting place.
callout: Compare the front of list A and list B, then attach the smaller node.
decision: Tail always points at the last node in the merged list so far.

narration: After attaching 1, compare the next front nodes and keep weaving the lists together.
callout: The merged list grows one node at a time in sorted order.
decision: Move tail forward after every attachment.

predictionPrompt.prompt: What should happen when one list runs out of nodes?

choices.label: Attach the rest of the other list
choices.feedback: Correct. The remaining nodes are already sorted.

choices.label: Start the merge again
choices.feedback: Not this one. The rest can be attached directly.

narration: Once one list ends, the rest of the other list can be attached right away.
callout: That is safe because the leftover nodes are already sorted.
decision: Return dummy.next, not the dummy itself.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is a dummy node helpful when building the merged list?
